verbatim_text: So in this section specifically, we're going to talk about failures that stem from under specified goals. I'm going to show how this reduces to a user modeling problem and how we can fix these errors through external scaffolding. So in the beginning of the talk, I listed a bunch of examples of failures of AI systems. And in this section, I'm going to focus on failures to infer specifications. So let's begin by defining the specification problem. So suppose the user prompts the language model with, give me an article. People often leave out crucial information in their prompts. So for example, there might be unstated preferences about what articles user likes to read. Or they might fail to specify details about the context for this request, like what they are reading for. So our solution to this problem is let's simply have the language model do the same thing. So our solution to this problem is let's simply have the language model interact with the user instead of relying on static prompts. So we do this by having language models proactively ask users questions. And we call this process gate or generative active task elicitation. And we're going to validate the effectiveness of interaction compared to static prompting.
